summary: A Jest test in test/config/get.ts failed because the code returned a registry URL with a trailing slash ('https://my-org.registry.example.com/') while the test asserted an exact match without a trailing slash ('https://my-org.registry.example.com'). This caused the jest process to exit with status 1, failing the GitHub Actions CI job.
root_cause: The test at test/config/get.ts:32 performs a strict equality check expecting 'https://my-org.registry.example.com' but the actual value returned was 'https://my-org.registry.example.com/' (with a trailing slash), causing the assertion to fail.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: Shows the exact value the test expected — no trailing slash
Shows the actual value returned by the code under test — includes a trailing slash, causing the mismatch
Identifies the exact line and assertion in test/config/get.ts that failed — a strict toBe equality check
Confirms the failure location is test/config/get.ts at line 32, column 38
Confirms this specific test file failed
Confirms the jest process exited non-zero due to the test failure

suggested_fix: Either update the assertion in test/config/get.ts:32 to accept a trailing slash (e.g., use .toBe('https://my-org.registry.example.com/') or strip trailing slashes before comparison), or fix the code under test to normalize registry URLs by trimming trailing slashes before returning them — depending on which behavior is intended.
